title: alnus rugosa f. tomophylla
intro: Alnus rugosa f. tomophylla, also known as the white alder or green alder, is a type of plant that is often confused with the white alder tree, but it is actually a shrub or small tree with smaller leaves. Alnus rugosa f. tomophylla has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Alnus rugosa f. tomophylla is typically prepared in the form of tea, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional herbal remedies, often in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
